paths.h(3) Library Functions Manual paths.h(3)

NAME

paths.h - System paths configuration (generated by CMake).

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define ASCIICHAT_INSTALL_PREFIX '/usr/local'
Installation prefix.

Detailed Description

System paths configuration (generated by CMake).

This file is generated from paths.h.in by CMake during the configuration step. It contains the installation prefix that is baked into the binary.

DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE DIRECTLY - edit paths.h.in instead.

Definition in file paths.h.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define ASCIICHAT_INSTALL_PREFIX '/usr/local'

Installation prefix. The base installation directory where ascii-chat was installed. The code uses this to construct platform-specific paths for system config and other resources.

Default install prefixes:

  • Linux: "/usr/local" (standard default)
  • macOS Intel (Homebrew): "/usr/local"
  • macOS ARM (Homebrew): "/opt/homebrew"
  • Windows: "C:\\Program Files\\ascii-chat"

System config path is constructed as:

  • Linux/macOS: "${INSTALL_PREFIX}/etc/ascii-chat/config.toml"
  • Windows: "${INSTALL_PREFIX}\\etc\\ascii-chat\\config.toml"

Definition at line 30 of file paths.h.

Referenced by get_discovery_database_dir().
